structured.description: A calibration algorithm for a kinova cube robot arm using a pybullet simulator and OpenCV library. The algorithm detects the corners of a green square in an image captured by a camera mounted on the robot arm, compares them to the real corners detected in a reference image, and calculates the error between the two sets of corners. The calibration process involves minimizing this error using a non-linear optimization method, and the results are written to a file for further analysis.

items.description: Calibrates a Kinova robot using a 4-corners method, detecting green corners in RGB images and calculating the error between real and virtual corners. It also minimizes the error using the Nelder-Mead algorithm to find the optimal position of the optical center.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: 1280 pixels, representing the width of the image captured by the camera.

attributes.name: error
attributes.description: Initialized to zero, it measures the difference between the virtual corners detected by the calibration process and the real corners of the cube. It's used for monitoring the progress of the calibration process.

attributes.name: real_corners
attributes.description: 4D, representing the real corners detected from the color camera images using the Kinova robot's green markers.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: Used to identify the specific robot being calibrated during the process.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 7-element vector representing the observation angles (in radians) for each of the 7 spherical joints of a Kinova robot.

items.name: Calibrator

items.description: Of the Calibrator class initializes the calibration parameters for a robotic arm based on a set of predefined observation angles.
items.name: __init__

items.description: Determines whether an approximate shape can be represented as a rectangle. It calculates the angle between four points and checks if all the cosine of these angles are below a threshold (0.1) indicating a rectangle-like shape. If all the cosine values are less than 0.1, the shape is considered a rectangle.

params.name: approx
params.description: Represented as a list of four numbers representing the vertices of a rectangle.

returns.description: True if the input approximation is a rectangle and False otherwise.

items.name: is_rectangle

items.description: Computes the angle between two points in a 2D space based on their distances and angles relative to a third point.

params.name: pt1
params.description: Represented by a list containing two elements, each representing the x and y coordinates of a point on the plane.

params.name: pt2
params.description: Defined as a point in space (x, y) representing the second point used to calculate the angle between two points.

params.name: pt0
params.description: Represented by a tuple containing two elements, representing the x and y coordinates of an arbitrary point.

returns.description: A measure of the angle between two points in a two-dimensional plane, calculated as the arctangent of the ratio of their distances from a reference point.

items.name: angle

items.description: Within the `Calibrator` class detects green corners in an image by converting it to HSV color space, finding contours using the moments algorithm, and then filtering and marking the corners with circles.

params.name: image
params.description: The input image to be processed, which is converted from BGR2 format to HSV for corner detection.

returns.description: A list of corner points detected from an image using HSV color space and morphological operations.

items.name: detect_green_corners

items.description: Calculates the distance between real and virtual corners, checks if they match, and computes the errors between them.

params.name: params
params.description: 7-dimensional, containing the first 3 elements representing real corners, the next 3 elements representing virtual corners, and the last element representing the fov.

returns.description: 1000 if there are fewer than 4 corners matched between the real and virtual sets, otherwise it returns 0.

items.name: error_function

items.description: Within the Calibrator class, takes in parameters and performs various operations related to calibration. It calculates offsets for position and orientation from initial values, prints out some information, reads a camera image, detects green corners, and compares them with real corners to calculate errors.

params.name: params
params.description: 7 elements long, containing 3 elements for com_p, 3 elements for com_o, and 1 element for fov.

returns.description: 2000 when there are fewer than 3 real corners and more than 7 virtual corners, otherwise it returns 0.

items.name: error_function2

items.description: Calculates the error between real and virtual corners, detects green corners, and returns the sum of errors.

params.name: params
params.description: Passed as an input to the function, containing the camera translation values.

returns.description: Not defined in the code snippet provided.

items.name: error_function3

items.description: Performs camera calibration for both PyBullet and Kinova cameras by detecting rectangles in the images, computing offsets, and displaying the detected rectangles on the original images.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Used to read the Kinova camera image during calibration.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate

items.description: Performs camera calibration for a Kinova robot by minimizing the error between the real and predicted corners using the Nelder-Mead algorithm, and updating the position and orientation of the camera based on the result.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: 4D array representing the input image for corner detection and calibration.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify a specific robot for calibration purposes.

items.name: calibrate2

items.description: Takes robot ID, image kinova and initial parameters as inputs. It calculates the fixed projection matrix, minimizes the error function to find the optimal camera translation, and detects green corners in the pybullet image.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot being calibrated.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: 8-bit RGB colored, representing an image captured by a Kinova camera.

items.name: calibrate3

items.description: Computes and retrieves an image from a camera using a view matrix and projection matrix, and rotates the image based on the camera's orientation.

returns.description: A numpy array of shape (1, 3, 2) representing the RGB color values of the camera image.

items.name: read_camera

items.description: Generates a fixed camera matrix and view matrix based on the camera's intrinsic and extrinsic parameters, and returns a color image captured by the camera.
items.name: read_camera_fixed

items.description: Within the Calibrator class processes an image and green corners to detect matches between real and virtual corners, calculating errors for each match.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to retrieve link state data from a robot with the given ID.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: An image that represents the green corners of the robot's kinematics.

items.name: cube_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class takes an image from a Kinova camera and a PyBullet image as input, detects rectangles in both images using the Canny edge detection algorithm and the contour area criterion, and matches the detected rectangles between the two images. It also calculates the distance between the corresponding corners of the matched rectangles and stores them in a list called `errors`.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot for which the image is being analyzed.

params.name: imageKinova
params.description: Passed as an input to the function, representing the image from which the rectangle contours will be detected.

items.name: square_test

items.description: Within the Calibrator class takes robot ID, kinova proxy, and camera proxy as inputs. It moves the joints of a Kinova arm to specific angles, captures color images using a camera, and saves them in a specified format.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to identify the robot for which kinova images are being generated.

params.name: kinova_proxy
params.description: Used to represent a proxy for communicating with the Kinova arm.

params.name: camera_proxy
params.description: Used to get images from a camera.

items.name: get_kinova_images

items.description: Performs calibration on a kinova robot arm by detecting and tracking corners in images captured by a camera mounted on the arm. It calculates the error between predicted and actual corner positions and returns the total error.

params.name: params
params.description: Passed as an array of two elements, representing the optical center X pixels and Y pixels respectively.

returns.description: The sum of norms of differences between virtual and real corners.

items.name: error_function4_square

items.description: Within the Calibrator class is responsible for detecting and matching green corners between a real-world image and a virtual cube, calculating the error between them, and returning the error value.

params.name: params
params.description: 2D, containing two elements: `optical_center_x_pixels` and `optical_center_y_pixels`.

returns.description: The sum of the norm of the difference between the detected virtual corners and the real corners.

items.name: error_function4_cube

items.description: Optimizes parameters for a calibration process, determining an optimal position for an optical center and computing an error value based on the position.

params.name: robot_id
params.description: Used to assign a unique identifier for the robot being calibrated.

items.name: calibrate4

structured.description: A robot arm with seven joints and calculates the inverse kinematics solution (joint positions) to reach a target position at the end effector of the arm. It utilizes the PyRobot package for Robot Operating System (ROS) to interface with the robot and perform calculations, as well as the numpy and math modules for mathematical operations. The code first initializes variables for the robot's ID, joint indices, and target position, then calculates the inverse kinematics solution using the calculateInverseKinematics method from PyRobot. Finally, it sets the motor control of each joint to move the end effector to the target position.

items.description: Manages a robot's movements based on data from sensors and buttons. It updates the robot's position and orientation using quaternion math and moves to the target position when the mode is "move". It also handles home commands for both the main robot and a secondary robot.

attributes.name: Period
attributes.description: 3 by default, which indicates that the worker will run for 3 consecutive cycles before stopping and resetting its state.

attributes.name: rgb
attributes.description: Used to store the RGB values for the robot's LED lights, which can be set or retrieved through the `set_rgb` method.

attributes.name: startup_check
attributes.description: 4 by default, which represents the startup check code for the robot's axis movement and gripper movement.

attributes.name: physicsClient
attributes.description: Used to interact with a physics engine client, allowing for simulated physics-based interactions between objects.

attributes.name: plane
attributes.description: A plane name, indicating which plane the worker operates on (e.g., "X-Y", "YZ", etc.).

attributes.name: robot_urdf
attributes.description: 3D robot model's URDF (Universal Robot Description Format) file, which defines the geometry and kinematics of the robot.

attributes.name: robot_launch_pos
attributes.description: A tuple of the robot's launch position (X, Y, Z coordinates).

attributes.name: robot_launch_orien
attributes.description: 4-element list representing the quaternion orientation of the robot's end effector in world coordinates for launching the gripper.

attributes.name: end_effector_link_index
attributes.description: 0-based indexing for the end effector links of a robot, indicating the index of the link in the robot's chain.

attributes.name: home_angles
attributes.description: Used to store the angles of the robot's joints when it is at its home position. It is used to compare with the angles read from the robot's encoders during movement to determine if the robot has reached its home position.

attributes.name: observation_angles
attributes.description: 3D angles representing the orientation of the worker's end effector, which are observed by the robot during its motion.

attributes.name: observation_angles_2
attributes.description: Used to store the current angle observation of the second robot arm in degrees. It is used to compute the desired position and orientation of the end effector based on the observation angles.

attributes.name: observation_angles_3
attributes.description: A list of 3 values representing the angles for the robot's gripper, X, Y, Z axes.

attributes.name: observation_angles_cube
attributes.description: Used to store the observation angles of the robot for each axis (X, Y, Z) in a cube format.

attributes.name: robot_id
attributes.description: 4 in this example, indicating that the robot being controlled is the fourth robot in a sequence of robots to be controlled by the worker.

attributes.name: cup
attributes.description: 0 by default, which means that the worker does not have a cup.

attributes.name: square
attributes.description: 4 by default, indicating that the worker can move in 4 directions (up, down, left, and right).

attributes.name: hilo_lectura
attributes.description: 0.01, which represents the incremental change in position or orientation of the robot's end effector when a button is pressed.

attributes.name: readDataFromProxy
attributes.description: Used to read data from a proxy server. It takes no arguments and returns a dictionary containing the data read from the proxy server.

attributes.name: hilo_camara
attributes.description: 14 characters long. It seems to be a camera ID or name, possibly for a specific robot or device.

attributes.name: readCamera
attributes.description: Not described in the code snippet provided. Therefore, I cannot provide a concise answer to this question based on the given code.

attributes.name: target_angles
attributes.description: 4D, representing the target angles for each axis (X, Y, Z, and gripper) as a list of floats in degrees.

attributes.name: target_position
attributes.description: Used to store the target position of the robot's end effector for each axis (X, Y, Z, and gripper). It contains the desired position of the end effector in a specific coordinate system.

attributes.name: target_orientation
attributes.description: Used to store the target orientation of the robot after a move command has been executed. It is updated with the new orientation value based on the `axis` attribute of the `data` list received as input.

attributes.name: target_velocities
attributes.description: A list of tuples containing the target velocity for each axis (X, Y, Z) and the gripper. The targets are the desired velocities of the robot in the specified axes and gripper movement.

attributes.name: joy_selected_joint
attributes.description: 0-based indexing of the selected joint to control the robot's movement based on the Joy input device.

attributes.name: move_mode
attributes.description: 0-based indexing for different move modes (e.g., home, move, or reset). It stores the current move mode selected by the user.

attributes.name: n_rotations
attributes.description: 3, indicating that the worker has 3 rotational degrees of freedom (axes).

attributes.name: ext_joints
attributes.description: A list of joints that are external to the robot, such as those on a gripper or end effector.

attributes.name: kinovaarm_proxy
attributes.description: Used to store a proxy for interacting with the KINOVA robot's API. It is set to the result of calling the `KinovaArmProxy()` function, which creates a Python interface for the KINOVA robot's API.

attributes.name: ext_gripper
attributes.description: 2D position of the gripper in the external reference frame, which is used to control the movement of the gripper.

attributes.name: posesTimes
attributes.description: 3D array containing the robot's pose (position and orientation) at different time steps, with each element representing a specific moment in time.

attributes.name: poses
attributes.description: 3D positions of the robot's end effector over time, used for positioning and orienting the robot's end effector to perform specific tasks.

attributes.name: timestamp
attributes.description: Used to keep track of the worker's current state, representing the number of milliseconds that have passed since the worker was created.

attributes.name: inicializar_toolbox
attributes.description: Responsible for initializing the `ToolBox` module of the PyRobot library, which provides tools to work with the robot's end effector and other features.

attributes.name: timer
attributes.description: 0 by default, it represents the time (in seconds) after which the worker will stop working if no new task is received.

attributes.name: compute
attributes.description: Defined as a function that takes a `data` argument, which is a `dict` object containing data from the Robot Operating System (ROS) message, and returns a value based on the data. The exact output of the `compute` attribute depends on the implementation of the class, but it is generally used to compute some value or perform some action based on the received data.

attributes.name: timer2
attributes.description: 2 seconds by default, which represents the time it takes for the worker to perform its task after the move mode has been triggered.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel
attributes.description: Responsible for moving the robot based on external velocity commands.

attributes.name: joint_speeds
attributes.description: 4D, representing the joint speeds as (joint name, axis, position, speed) tuples for each joint in the robot. It is used to specify the desired joint positions and speeds during movement.

attributes.name: gains
attributes.description: Representing the gains (or sensitivity) of each axis of the robot's end effector, which determines how much the robot moves when a button is pressed.

attributes.name: speeds
attributes.description: 0-dimensional, representing a list of target positions for each axis of the robot, which are used to control the robot's movement.

attributes.name: angles
attributes.description: Used to store the angles of the robot's axes in radians.

attributes.name: timer3
attributes.description: 3 seconds by default, representing the time it takes for the worker to perform its actions.

attributes.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds
attributes.description: 3-dimensional array that contains the target positions for the Kinova robot's end effectors, along with their speeds, in the x, y, and z axes.

attributes.name: timer4
attributes.description: 4, indicating that this worker has a timer for move mode 4.

attributes.name: movePybulletWithToolbox
attributes.description: A method that moves the robot based on data from the PyBullet API and the Toolbox API, updating the target position and orientation of the robot.

attributes.name: colorKinova
attributes.description: 4 characters long, representing the color of the Kinova gripper.

attributes.name: depthKinova
attributes.description: 0-based indexing of the depth sensor of a Kinova robot arm, indicating the position of the end effector in the robot's workspace.

attributes.name: calibrator
attributes.description: Used to store a reference to the calibration data for the specific worker. It is used to read the calibration data from the file and to update the target position and orientation based on the calibration data.

attributes.name: timer5
attributes.description: 5 by default, representing the time (in seconds) for which the worker will stay in move mode before returning to home position.

attributes.name: readKinovaCamera
attributes.description: Used to read data from a Kinova camera. It takes no arguments and returns a tuple containing the camera's data.

items.name: SpecificWorker

items.description: Sets up the necessary components for the SpecificWorker to operate, including a Kinova arm proxy, a timer for controlling the movement of the robot, and various variables for storing information about the robot's position, orientation, velocities, and more.

params.name: proxy_map
params.description: Used to define a mapping between the robot's joint angles and the corresponding joint speeds for each joint. This mapping is used to compute the joint speeds that will move the robot to a desired position and orientation.

params.name: startup_check
params.description: Used to check if the kinova arm is properly connected and calibrated before starting the worker thread.

params.name: params
params.description: Used to set parameters for the function.

returns.description: True if successful, otherwise it raises an exception.

items.name: setParams

items.description: Performs tasks related to observing angles, moving Kinova with angles, and calibrating the robot's end effector. It also handles observation angles and square test for Yolo detector.
items.name: compute

items.description: Tests various components and interfaces of a RoboCompKinovaArm, including TPose, TGripper, TJoint, TJoints, AxisParams, ButtonParams, and TData. It also sets a timer to quit the application after 200 milliseconds.
items.name: startup_check

items.description: Initializes the worker's toolbox by creating an environment, adding grippers and objects, and setting up axes and goal points. It also defines the worker's arrival point and time step size.
items.name: inicializar_toolbox

items.description: Computes and updates the joint states of a robot based on its target velocities, while also handling the limits of the joints' angles.
items.name: toolbox_compute

items.description: Computes and updates the view matrix, projection matrix, and camera vector based on the robot's position and orientation. It then captures an image using the Segmentation Mask Object and displays it in a window.
items.name: readCamera

returns.description: 1-dimensional numpy array representing a boolean value indicating whether the operation was successful or not.

items.description: Calculates and prints the radian values of the joints of a Kinova robot.
items.name: showKinovaAngles

items.description: Computes camera calibration for a Kinova manipulator using a Chessboard pattern. It captures images, detects chessboard corners, and computes the intrinsic parameters of the camera using the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) algorithm. The resulting camera matrix and distortion coefficients are saved to a file.

returns.description: 1 if the camera calibration was successful and 0 otherwise.

items.name: get_kinova_instrinsic

items.description: Provides a brief message and four options for user interaction: taking a picture, computing calibration, resetting the program, or quitting.
items.name: usage

items.name: movePybulletWithExternalVel

items.description: Sets joint motor control velocity for a robot with toolbox.
items.name: movePybulletWithToolbox

items.description: Continuously retrieves joints state and gripper state data from an external proxy, scales the gripper distance by 0.8, and sleeps for 0.05 seconds between updates.
items.name: readDataFromProxy

items.description: Moves the kinova arm based on angles specified by the user, rounding the angle values to the nearest degree and passing them to the `moveJointsWithAngle` method of the kinova arm proxy.

params.name: angles
params.description: Rounded to the nearest degree (in radians) between 0 and 360 before being assigned to a list.

items.name: moveKinovaWithSpeeds
